policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Seven consecutive clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-19 each passed all 1723 tests over the identical complete 27592-line assembly scope; six runs measured 19219 covered lines and one measured 19220 (69.658%), so 19220 is the reviewed high-water. Because the stale-baseline gate has no high-side tolerance, pinning the lower 19219 reading made the +1 Coverlet variance intermittently exit 1 (stale-baseline); the high-water baseline plus the unchanged two-line low-side tolerance now absorbs the observed 19218-19220 instrumentation range without either a stale-baseline or a regression failure. The variance originates in issue #157 (BI-SRV-096): the auto-movie, auto-season and watchlist monitors reconcile their event subscriptions against the live configuration on every ConfigurationChanged fan-out (SeerrIntegrationPolicy.InvalidateCachedActiveState now notifies all three monitors), so a feature toggled on after startup acquires its subscription without a restart and a toggled-off feature releases it; PlaybackWatcherBase gains a terminal disposed guard mirroring WatchlistMonitor. New MonitorSubscriptionLifecycleTests drive the production LiveNotifierService config-change path and assert real event invocation-list counts for hot-enable, hot-disable, idempotent repeats, master-switch override, disposal, and unavailable-configuration release; their threaded config-change paths are the source of the single-line hit variance. Relative to the reviewed 19150/27560 baseline, the reviewed assembly scope grows by 32 instrumented lines and covered lines advance by 70 while line coverage rises from 69.485% to 69.658%. coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
